docs.title: ⚡ MURPHY: THE HIGH-SPEED CODING PREDATOR
docs.text: Surgical Precision. Extreme Speed. Zero Hesitation.

docs.title: 🦅 Mission Objective
docs.text:
Murphy is not just another coding assistant. It is an Agentic Predator designed to surpass Claude Code, Codex, and existing alternatives through a dual-model orchestration pipeline, parallelized tool execution, and an unbreakable self-healing loop.

Predator v3.2 is Online

The latest build features Atomic Session Persistence, Interactive TUI Navigation, and NVIDIA NIM optimized execution.

docs.title: ⚡ Why Murphy Dominates
docs.text: Capability The Competition MURPHY Orchestration Single Model Kimi K2 + Qwen3-Coder Execution Sequential Parallel Pipeline (Promise.all) Stability Stops on Error Self-Healing Loop Interface Basic Text Living Hierarchy TUI Memory Volatile Atomic JSON Persistence

docs.title: 🏗️ Core Architecture
docs.text:
graph TD
    User([User Request]) --> Engine[Dual-Model Engine]
    subgraph Engine
        Kimi[Kimi K2 Strategic Planner]
        Qwen[Qwen3 Surgical Operator]
    end
    Kimi -->|Plan| Qwen
    Qwen -->|Parallel Tools| Pipeline[Execution Pipeline]
    Pipeline -->|Result| TUI[Living Hierarchy UI]
    TUI -->|Iteration| User

docs.title: 🔥 Next Steps
docs.text:
• Install the Predator
  • Master Operation
  • Explore the Tools

docs.title: 🧠 Predator Anatomy: Architecture
docs.text: Murphy's dominance is mathematically derived from its multi-layer architecture.

docs.title: 🏗️ The Dual-Model Brain
docs.text: Murphy separates "Thinking" from "Doing" to prevent the logical stalls common in single-model agents.

docs.title: 1. The Strategic Core (Kimi K2)
docs.text: Kimi K2 functions as the General. It does not code; it plans. It analyzes the entire codebase and defines the path forward. - Strength: Long-context reasoning & massive planning trees. - Latency: Sub-30s planning cycles.

docs.title: 2. The Surgical Blade (Qwen3-Coder)
docs.text: Qwen3-Coder functions as the Operator. It receives orders from Kimi and executes them with surgical precision. - Strength: Elite coding ability & tool-calling fidelity. - Execution: Real-time streaming output.

docs.title: 🔗 The Unbreakable Engine
docs.text:
Even if a model hallucinations a tool call, Murphy has a redundant Text-to-Tool Parser.

graph LR
    A[Model Output] --> B{Valid JSON?}
    B -- Yes --> C[Execute Direct]
    B -- No --> D[Activate Redundant Parser]
    D --> E[Regex Extraction]
    E --> F[Tool Execution]

docs.title: 🚄 Parallelized Pipeline
docs.text: Unlike other agents that read files one-by-one, Murphy uses Promise.all to saturate its I/O bandwidth. - Batch Processing: Up to 10 concurrent tools. - Zero Stall: If one file fails, the rest continue.

docs.title: 💾 Session Intelligence
docs.text: Every mission is persisted to .murphy_session.json using Atomic Writes. - Resilience: Even if the power fails, your history survives. - Pruning: Murphy automatically manages token context to keep your missions fast and cheap.

docs.title: 🖥️ UI Stack
docs.text:
• React + Ink: The core of the Living Hierarchy TUI.
  • Error Boundaries: Native React crash protection ensuring Murphy stays online during extreme bugs.

docs.title: 🤝 Tactical Contribution: Development
docs.text: Join the elite team building the future of autonomous coding.

docs.title: 🏗️ Development Setup
docs.text:
1. Fork \& Clone the target repository.
  2. Install Base: npm install
  3. Build Engine: npm run build
  4. Launch Watcher: npm run dev (Hot reloads the TUI)

docs.title: 📜 Coding Standards
docs.text:
• TypeScript: Mandatory strict typing.
  • Prettier: Use our .prettierrc for formatting.
  • ESLint: Ensure your code passes mission-ready linting.

docs.title: 🧪 Intelligence Testing
docs.text:
Before pushing changes, run the test suite:

npm test

We use Vitest for high-speed unit testing of tool handlers and the agent loop.

docs.title: 🚀 Deployment Pipeline
docs.text: Murphy uses GitHub Actions for automatic deployment. - Pushing to main triggers a doc build and deploy to GitHub Pages. - Tool updates are automatically verified against the TUI.

docs.title: 🦅 Acknowledgments
docs.text: Special thanks to: - NVIDIA NIM for the GPU-accelerated brains. - Moonshot AI \& Alibaba for the elite models. - The Ink Community for the TUI foundation.

docs.title: 🛠️ Mission Targeting: Installation
docs.text: Getting Murphy operational on your machine is a high-speed process. Choose your deployment vector.

docs.title: ⚡ Deployment Vectors
docs.text:
NPX (Instant)Global CLI (Permanent)Source (Dev Mode)

Run Murphy without installation: 

npx @pranav271103/murphycode

Install Murphy as a system-wide threat: 

npm install -g @pranav271103/murphycode
murphycode

Clone the predator for deep configuration: 

git clone https://github.com/pranav271103/Murphy.git
cd Murphy
npm install
npm run build
npm start

docs.title: 🔑 Credential Acquisition
docs.text:
Murphy requires an NVIDIA NIM API Key to fuel its dual-model brain.

  1. Navigate to build.nvidia.com
  2. Generate a new API Key.
  3. Inject it into your environment:
# Set locally
export NVIDIA_API_KEY=your_key_here

# Or create a .env file in the Murphy root
NVIDIA_API_KEY=your_key_here

Permission Requirement

Ensure your user has read/write permissions for the directory Murphy will operate in.

docs.title: 🖥️ System Compatibility
docs.text:
• Node.js: 18.x or higher
  • OS: Windows (PowerShell/CMD), macOS, Linux
  • Resources: Minimal; all heavy computation is offloaded to NVIDIA NIM.

docs.title: 🔨 Tactical Tools: The Arsenal
docs.text: Murphy is equipped with a high-performance toolset for project manipulation.

docs.title: 📂 File System Operations
docs.text: Tool Capability Parameters read_file High-speed content retrieval path, offset, limit write_file Atomic file creation path, content edit_file Surgical code replacement path, old_string, new_string delete_file Target termination path list_directory Reconnaissance path, recursive, pattern create_directory Base expansion path

docs.title: run_command
docs.text:
The primary tool for execution. In Windows missions, Murphy automatically selects PowerShell for maximum compatibility.

  • Timeout: Hard cap of 2 minutes (configurable).
  • Hardening: Prevents OOM by truncating massive outputs.
  • Interruption: Linked to AbortSignal for instant cancellation.

docs.title: grep & glob
docs.text: Standard searching is too slow. Murphy uses optimized pattern matching to find targets across thousands of files in milliseconds.

docs.title: fetch_url
docs.text: Web-enabled reconnaissance. Use this to read documentation from the web or fetch external assets. - SSRF Protected: Explicitly blocks access to internal networks.

docs.title: 🚦 Safety Protocols
docs.text:
Every tool call is wrapped in a Permission Layer.

Destructive Action

Actions like delete_file or run_command will ALWAYS prompt for a [y/N] confirmation unless configured otherwise. 

⚠️ PERMISSION REQUIRED: run_command
{"command": "rm -rf ./"}
Allow? [Y]es / [N]o

docs.title: ⚡ Operational Manual: Usage
docs.text: Mastering Murphy requires understanding the interface and the execution flow.

docs.title: 🏎️ Launching the Interface
docs.text:
Once launched, you are greeted by the Predator TUI. This is where missions are defined and executed.

murphycode

docs.title: ⌨️ Tactical Keyboard Controls
docs.text: Key Action Enter Submit mission parameters ↑ / ↓ Navigate command history ESC Abort current task execution Ctrl + L Instant screen wipe (clears UI) Ctrl + C Force exit predator

docs.title: 📜 Mission Command Intel (Slash Commands)
docs.text:
Murphy supports direct tactical commands to manage the state.

Command Action /new Resets the agent and clears current context /clear Wipes only the visual message history /help Displays the command intelligence overview exit Closes the platform

docs.title: 🧠 Strategic Execution Flow
docs.text:
When you provide a request, Murphy enters a high-speed iteration loop:

  1. Reasoning Phase (Kimi K2): Strategic breakdown of your request.
  2. Permission Step: If a dangerous tool (disk write/shell) is needed, Murphy halts for a [y/N] confirmation. (1)
  3. Execution Phase (Qwen3): Surgical tool usage.
  4. Verification Phase: Checking results to ensure mission success.

{ .annotate }

  1. Safety First: You can whitelist "Safe Commands" in the configuration to skip this step for read-only tasks.

docs.title: 🛠️ Advanced Operation: Global Access
docs.text:
Since Murphy v3.2, you can jump into any folder and launch:

cd /path/to/any/project
murphy

Murphy will automatically detect the local context and begin reconnaissance.
